explain.rule: Wish and if only express regrets, complaints, and desires. Three uses: (1) Wish + past simple = desire for present to be different: 'I wish I knew the answer' (I don't know now). (2) Wish + past perfect = regret about the past: 'I wish I had studied harder' (I didn't — regret). (3) Wish + would = wish someone would change their behaviour: 'I wish you would stop.' If only = same as wish but stronger/more emotional.
explain.why_matters: Express regrets, unfulfilled wishes, and complaints naturally. Common in everyday conversation and essential at B2.
explain.hebrew_note: Hebrew uses 'לו רק' or 'אם רק' similarly to 'if only'. The tense logic mirrors conditional structures. Key: wish + PAST SIMPLE for present wish (not present tense). Wish + PAST PERFECT for past regret.
explain.tip: Three tenses, three meanings. Wish + PAST SIMPLE = present wish (I don't have it now). Wish + PAST PERFECT = past regret (I didn't do it then). Wish + WOULD = future behaviour complaint (stop doing that!). Never: 'I wish I know' or 'I wish I studied' for present — needs past simple 'I wish I knew'.

good_examples.sentence: I wish I knew the answer.
good_examples.audio_text: I wish I knew the answer.
good_examples.note: Present wish — I don't know the answer now

good_examples.sentence: If only it weren't so cold!
good_examples.audio_text: If only it weren't so cold!
good_examples.note: If only = stronger wish/complaint about present

good_examples.sentence: I wish I had studied harder.
good_examples.audio_text: I wish I had studied harder.
good_examples.note: Past regret — I didn't study hard

good_examples.sentence: If only I hadn't said that.
good_examples.audio_text: If only I hadn't said that.
good_examples.note: Past regret — I did say it and I regret it

good_examples.sentence: I wish you would stop interrupting me.
good_examples.audio_text: I wish you would stop interrupting me.

good_examples.sentence: She wishes she lived closer to the city.
good_examples.audio_text: She wishes she lived closer to the city.
good_examples.note: Present wish — she lives far away

good_examples.sentence: I wish I had taken that opportunity when I had the chance.
good_examples.audio_text: I wish I had taken that opportunity when I had the chance.
good_examples.note: Regret about a missed past opportunity

good_examples.sentence: They wish they had saved more money when they were young.
good_examples.audio_text: They wish they had saved more money when they were young.

good_examples.sentence: I wish it would stop raining — we've been stuck inside all day.
good_examples.audio_text: I wish it would stop raining — we've been stuck inside all day.

bad_examples.wrong: I wish I know the answer.
bad_examples.correct: I wish I knew the answer.
bad_examples.reason: After wish for present wishes, use past simple — not present simple.

bad_examples.wrong: I wish I studied harder last year.
bad_examples.correct: I wish I had studied harder last year.
bad_examples.reason: For past regrets, use past perfect after wish: had studied.

bad_examples.correct: I wish you would stop talking.
bad_examples.reason: For complaints about behaviour, use would after wish.

bad_examples.wrong: If only I would know the answer.
bad_examples.correct: If only I knew the answer.
bad_examples.reason: Don't use would for present wishes about yourself — use past simple.

bad_examples.wrong: She wishes she lives in a bigger flat.
bad_examples.correct: She wishes she lived in a bigger flat.
bad_examples.reason: Present wish → past simple after wish. Lives → lived.

exercises.question: I wish I ___ (know) the answer to that question.
exercises.hint: Present wish → past simple after wish
exercises.explanation: wish + past simple for present wish.
exercises.hebrew_note: I wish + עבר פשוט = משאלה בהווה

exercises.question: If only it ___ (not/be) so expensive!
exercises.explanation: If only + weren't (past simple of be for wishes).

exercises.question: I wish I ___ (study) harder when I was younger.
exercises.explanation: wish + past perfect for past regret.

exercises.question: If only I ___ (not/say) that — it was so rude.
exercises.explanation: hadn't said — past perfect negative.

exercises.question: I wish you ___ (stop) making so much noise.
exercises.explanation: wish + would for behaviour complaints.

exercises.question: She wishes she ___ (live) closer to her family.
exercises.explanation: lived — past simple after wish.

exercises.question: If only we ___ (take) that opportunity when we had the chance.
exercises.explanation: had taken — past perfect.

exercises.question: He wishes he ___ (can) speak another language.
exercises.hint: Present wish about ability → could (past of can)
exercises.explanation: could — past simple of can for wishes.

exercises.question: I wish it ___ (stop) raining — we've been inside all day.
exercises.explanation: would stop — future behaviour/change.

exercises.question: If only I ___ (be) taller — I can't reach the top shelf.
exercises.hint: Present wish about physical state → were
exercises.explanation: were — past simple of be for all subjects in wishes.

exercises.question: I wish I ___ the answer.
exercises.explanation: knew — past simple after wish for present wish.

exercises.question: She wishes she ___ more time to travel.
exercises.explanation: had — past simple after wish.

exercises.question: I wish you ___ so much noise!
exercises.explanation: wouldn't make — negative would for behaviour complaint.
exercises.distractors: don't make / hadn't made / won't make

exercises.question: If only I ___ taken that job offer.
exercises.explanation: had taken — past perfect for past regret.

exercises.question: Which expresses a present wish?
exercises.explanation: lived = past simple after wish = present wish.
exercises.distractors: I wish I had lived closer. / I wish I will live closer. / I wish I live closer.

exercises.question: He wishes he ___ harder when he was at school.
exercises.explanation: had worked — past perfect.

exercises.question: If only the weather ___ a bit warmer!
exercises.explanation: were — past simple of be for wishes.

exercises.question: I wish you ___ your phone during dinner.
exercises.explanation: wouldn't use — negative would for complaint.
exercises.distractors: don't use / hadn't used / won't use

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'I wish I know the answer.'
exercises.answer: I wish I knew the answer.
exercises.hint: Present wish → past simple after wish
exercises.explanation: knew — past simple, not present.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'I wish I studied harder last year.'
exercises.answer: I wish I had studied harder last year.
exercises.explanation: had studied — past perfect for past regret.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'I wish you stop talking.'
exercises.answer: I wish you would stop talking.
exercises.explanation: would stop — wish + would for behaviour.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'She wishes she lives in a bigger flat.'
exercises.answer: She wishes she lived in a bigger flat.
exercises.explanation: lived — past simple after wish.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: I wish I knew the answer.
exercises.hint: Wish + past simple for present wish
exercises.explanation: wish + knew (past simple).

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: If only I hadn't said that.
exercises.hint: If only + past perfect for past regret
exercises.explanation: hadn't said — past perfect.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: I wish you would stop interrupting.
exercises.explanation: would stop — complaint about behaviour.

speak.sentence: I wish I had learnt to play the piano when I was young.
chunks: I wish I had learnt to play the piano

speak.sentence: If only I hadn't said that — I feel terrible about it now.
chunks: If only I hadn't said that —
I feel terrible about it now.

speak.sentence: I wish you would tidy your room — it's a complete mess.
chunks: I wish you would tidy your room —

speak.sentence: She wishes she lived closer to her family.
If only we had booked earlier — the prices have gone up so much.
chunks: If only we had booked earlier —
the prices have gone up so much.

speak.sentence: I wish I could speak another language fluently.
chunks: I wish I could speak another language

speak.sentence: He wishes he had taken more risks when he was younger.
chunks: He wishes he had taken more risks

speak.sentence: If only the meeting would start on time for once!
chunks: If only the meeting would start on time
